recent_news.title: The Importance of the Battle of Savo Island
recent_news.summary: On the night of Aug. 9, 1942, an Allied fleet of 17 warships guarded the approaches to Guadalcanal. The fleet was newer, larger, and better equipped than the Japanese force bearing down on it. It had six heavy cruisers, two light cruisers, and nine destroyers. It carried radar, a technology that should have detected the enemy long before any lookout could spot a ship through the darkness. By the numbers, the Allied squadron was on average 10 years newer and outweighed its opponent by more than 8

recent_news.title: A Formal Defense Pact in the Indo-Pacific Is the Wrong Answer
recent_news.summary: The debate over how best to deter China in the western Pacific has reached a new level of ambition. Ely Ratner, a former senior defense official in the Biden administration, proposed a “Pacific Defense Pact” — a legally binding multilateral treaty among the United States, Japan, Australia, and the Philippines. recent_news.title: Japan's Tokyu to trial data centers along Tokyo railway line
recent_news.summary: Company says railway line already has ample fiber connectivity
recent_news.body_text:
DCD Magazine #60 is out now! Download and read for free here
Company says railway line already has ample fiber connectivity
Japanese rail conglomerate Tokyu is exploring deploying data centers along one of its railway routes.
Tokyu Corporation, Tokyu Electric Railway Co., iTSCOM, and Tokyu Construction Co., last week announced they will start a demonstration experiment to develop urban data center
